position_summary: Claude’s outline has the right sharp spine, and most headline funnel/NAB2/STAT6 numbers I checked are supported by repository artifacts. The largest problems are not the central numeric funnel but two paper-threatening truth issues: the unresolved dataset DOI conflict and a ledger claim/source mismatch around CTLA4; method framing also risks overstating independence because the program hop is partly preselected and the full CS generation stage used cached-receipt replay rather than wholly live generation.

findings.title: C23 DOI conflict is unresolved and paper-blocking
findings.evidence: C23 explicitly flags a conflict in docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:185. references/README.md:5-6 says bioRxiv URL/DOI 10.1101/2025.12.23.696273, while README.md:77-78 and MEMORY.md:19-20 say doi:10.64898/2025.12.23.696273. This is a repo-confirmed mismatch, not a reviewer nuance.
findings.concern_path: The manuscript or data availability section cites the wrong identifier, causing citation-resolution failure or an obvious bibliographic correction request during review.
findings.suggested_action: Resolve against the authoritative preprint landing page before drafting; update all repo references to one DOI and keep C23 open until fixed.

findings.title: C17 ledger claim mixes CTLA4 sources and is unsupported by the named perturbseq-qc source
findings.evidence: C17 says EGR2/GATA3 YES, IL2 UNTESTED, SLC1A5/CTLA4 REFUTED with source WORK_PROGRESS M2 plus docs/perturbseq-qc_2026-07-07 (docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:179). In the named perturbseq-qc folder, SLC1A5 refutation is supported by docs/perturbseq-qc_2026-07-07/pyzobot_referee_results.md:36-40, and IL2 0/2 guides UNTESTED is supported at lines 32-34. But CTLA4 is not refuted there; docs/perturbseq-qc_2026-07-07/pyzobot_referee_ranked_full.csv:154 and :164 show CTLA4 supported for autoimmune disease at Stim8hr/Stim48hr. CTLA4 REFUTED appears instead in docs/perturbseq-e2e-autorun_2026-07-07/referee_writeup.md:92-104, a different artifact not listed as C17’s source.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer or internal checker opens the claimed source and sees CTLA4 supported, not refuted, making the ledger look cherry-picked or sloppy even though a CTLA4 Rest refutation exists elsewhere.
findings.suggested_action: Either cite the actual CTLA4 Rest e2e artifact with condition/disease scope, or remove CTLA4 from the C17 inventory and ledger unless it is regenerated in the current referee artifacts.

findings.title: The verdict ledger is too small to prove referee discrimination as a method claim
findings.evidence: The outline elevates the ledger as proof that the referee discriminates (docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:17-26, :88-96, :136). The repo supports examples: EGR2/IL2/SLC1A5 in docs/perturbseq-qc_2026-07-07/pyzobot_referee_results.md:25-40, funnel classes in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json:17-25, and replication caveat that referee alone supports 395/47,220 while literature/OpenTargets culls to 43 at docs/replication_report_2026-07-08.md:56-58. But these are example verdicts plus a funnel, not a held-out precision/recall benchmark or calibration curve.
findings.concern_path: FRMA/LBD reviewers accept the ledger as an illustrative audit trail but reject the stronger inference that the referee’s discrimination has been validated methodologically.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the NO/UNTESTED examples sharp, but phrase the ledger as demonstration evidence unless a small benchmark, negative-control panel, or sensitivity analysis is added.

findings.title: Program-hop independence is overstated because A-universe preselects program-significant genes
findings.evidence: The code builds A with KD gate + effect and, when program_significant=True, intersects with genes significant for the Th1/Th2 program: src/arbiter/lbd/entities.py:47-55 and :86-87. The Stage 1 run used program_significant=true in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json:11. The replication report already warns: 'The program-significance filter creates a tautology inside the funnel' at docs/replication_report_2026-07-08.md:52-55.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer sees a 4-hop receipt chain but realizes hop 2 was partly baked into candidate eligibility, so the chain can be accused of double-counting support for the program hop.
findings.suggested_action: Make the tautology caveat visible in Methods/Results, not buried only in Limitations; do not present the program hop as independent evidence for funnel survivors.

findings.title: CS-native generation claim risks overstating the full-pipeline run’s liveness
findings.evidence: The outline says CS authored + ran the generator/referee natively (docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:82-86, :102-106). The repo says Stage 1 was a full 3,935-gene sweep, but as cached-receipt replay: docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md:42-43 and mapping table line 95. The live authorship claim is supported separately only by a 12-gene live microsweep where NAB2 did not appear: docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md:109-122.
findings.concern_path: The paper’s headline says the workbench closes the loop live, but the hero full-scale generation evidence is a replay of cached receipts; a reviewer reads this as post-hoc instrumentation theater unless the distinction is explicit.
findings.suggested_action: Separate claims: full-scale CS reproduction via cached-receipt replay; live CS authorship proven by microsweep; external replication remains outside CS by design.

findings.title: Several high-value numbers check out, but the outline should cite primary artifacts rather than summaries
findings.evidence: Matches verified: C1-C8/C5/C6/C7 in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json:7, :14-25; C9-C10 in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage1/lbd_questions_Stim8hr.json:48-58; C11-C14 in docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md:33-36 and replication_report.md:22-24; C15-C16 in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage3/stage3_cis.json:6-17 and :47-50 plus receipt.md:12-20; C18-C20 in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md:77-87; C21 in docs/replication_report_2026-07-08.md:3-5, :15-26, :33-37. However, some outline sources point to prose summaries when JSON/receipt artifacts are cleaner.
findings.concern_path: Numbers are mostly defensible, but a manuscript build from prose summaries will be harder to audit and more likely to drift during editing.
findings.suggested_action: For every C1-C21 claim, cite the JSON/receipt line-bearing artifact as source of truth, with prose docs only as narrative corroboration.

findings.title: C15/STAT6 language still contains overclaiming in source artifacts despite the planned calibration story
findings.evidence: The calibrated-language story is supported by docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage5/review.json, which flags 'validated' and 'definitive' as violations. But repo source artifacts still use strong language: docs/nab2_stat6_definitive_check.py:1 and :7-8 say 'DEFINITIVE' and 'definitively'; docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md:80 and :94-99 say 'DEFINITIVELY REFUTED/EXCLUDED' and 'genuine'.
findings.concern_path: The paper claims a critic enforced calibrated language, while cited source files still contain the exact kind of language the critic supposedly removed. A reviewer can read this as cosmetic self-audit rather than discipline.
findings.suggested_action: Use the critic event as a visible edit history, but ensure manuscript-facing quotes and figure labels use 'refuted' / 'consistent with NAB2 perturbation' rather than 'definitive' / 'genuine'.

findings.title: Perturb-seq-as-oracle is too strong for the disease hop
findings.evidence: The outline calls the Perturb-seq substrate a held experimental oracle at docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:72-74. The finding doc itself warns the atopic-eczema label comes from GWAS gene assignment with no colocalization/LD control at docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md:77-78 and says the disease label is genetic-association evidence at :98-99. The README similarly scopes the claim: README.md:41 says disease label is Open Targets genetic-association evidence and NAB2 stays a nomination.
findings.concern_path: The wet-lab reader accepts perturbation support for NAB2 as a Th1/Th2 regulator but objects that disease support is not experimental causality, so 'oracle' sounds like overclaiming.
findings.suggested_action: Reserve 'held experimental substrate' for KD/effect/program receipts; call the disease hop an enrichment/association receipt, not an oracle for disease causality.

findings.title: Actor-critic independence is narrower than the outline implies
findings.evidence: The model cast is repo-supported: docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md:28 and :77-87 say OPERON=Opus 4.8 and Reviewer=Sonnet 5; MEMORY.md:98 also records inline Haiku 4.5. But both actor and critic are Anthropic-family Claude models, and cross-model independence is explicitly external, not CS-native, in docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/README.md:99.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer may reject 'independent critic model' if it reads as independent platform/model family rather than independent role/checkpoint inside the same vendor system.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the self-audit claim, but define independence operationally: separate reviewer role/model/checkpoint, with Codex replication as external cross-model audit.

findings.title: Henry/R01 narrative may invite a higher validation burden than the outline currently satisfies
findings.evidence: The outline centers the R01 critique at docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:55-58 and says Wayfinder answers it. The replication report simultaneously cautions that 43 supported is a joint gate x referee product, not referee-only, and that the disease FDR provenance is inherited: docs/replication_report_2026-07-08.md:56-60.
findings.concern_path: By quoting 'almost none get followed up,' the paper sets up reviewers to ask whether this work actually follows up hypotheses or only retrospectively adjudicates one dataset with inherited disease-enrichment statistics.
findings.suggested_action: Frame the answer as 'follow-up triage/adjudication against a held substrate' rather than a complete replacement for prospective experimental follow-up.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Address the two P0s first: resolve the dataset DOI and repair or re-source C17/CTLA4. Then decide how explicitly the outline will disclose the program-hop tautology and cached-replay boundary without sanding off the agentic-loop/self-audit claims.
